The question is really very simple: Do you believe all eligible Americans have the right to vote or are you terrified of what those voters will do to you if they are able to exercise that right?

Because, Harmeet, we have a Constitution that leaves that up to the states. As a good conservative who supports the Court’s decision in Dobbs among other things, I’m sure you’ve heard of states’ rights.


Democracy Docket (@DemocracyDocket)

NEW: As SCOTUS weighs a case that could toss millions of legal mail ballots, the DOJ is pushing to restrict voting to a "single day."

Civil Rights AAG Harmeet Dhillon: “The rest of the world has elections...on a single day. Why can’t America do it?”

Read m

A very wise man once told me you could (& should) only hire very smart people to be federal prosecutors. But that’s easy to figure out. He explained what you’re really hiring for is good judgment. And with all due respect, that takes some time to develop.


Ben Penn (@benjaminpenn)

After President Trump claimed to reporters twice today that he had recently spoken to a former president who praised his actions in Iran, saying: “I wish I did what you did.” Aides to all four living presidents, Bill Clinton, George W. Bush, Barack Obama and Joe Biden, have issued statements to CNN, saying there is no record of any communications between them and President Trump.

Trump and his FCC Chair Brendan Carr are attacking the 1st Amendment again. It would be easy to miss this story in the press of the news, but our continued exercise of our 1st Amendment rights & advocacy for the principle itself are essential to its survival—it’s a fundamental threat to Trump’s ability to accumulate more power. open.substack.com/pub/joycev…

Could a Democrat take MTG's seat in Georgia? Shawn Harris, the retired General who came in 1st in the special election & now faces a runoff, made a believer of me when I met him this morning. Listening to him talk about what country over party looks like is refreshing. open.substack.com/pub/joycev…

Glad you feel that way. We really enjoy doing it and it forces us to think critically.

So, Geoff Bennett has a new book, Black Out Loud, & it’s nothing you’re expecting and everything you want to read write now, so I got him to talk with me about it & about what it’s like to be in the anchor chair for NewsHour.

Farmer and retired Brigadier General Shawn Harris, the Georgia Democrat in the runoff to replace MTG in Congress, joins us for coffee Saturday morning. His background makes him a qualified & compelling candidate. Leave your questions for him in the chat and join us! open.substack.com/pub/joycevan...

On a Thursday in America…
joycevance.substack.com/p/thursday-i...
I really don’t care…

Today, Justice Barrett said “Freedom of speech & freedom of religion commit us to pluralism. They commit us to tolerance. They…allow to be heard even those viewpoints that we might disagree with.”
So, what does she make of Trump, who lacks that commitment? open.substack.com/pub/joycevan...

When the President decides photojournalists can’t take pictures at Sec’y Hegseth’s Iran briefings, it’s not a small thing. We’re connecting the dots to the larger efforts to erode our 1st Amendment rights, just when we need them the most. open.substack.com/pub/joycevan...

One of the law firms Trump tried to intimidate with an executive order is representing Anthropic in court. Here are the details of the two lawsuits against the government after DOD tried to blacklist the developer of Claude AI. 
open.substack.com/pub/joycevan...

The corruption, waste, and greed of this entire administration is beyond cataloguing. But @joycewhitevance.bsky.social does a nice job skewering our new Special Envoy for the Shield of the Americas. Laugh or cry. open.substack.com/pub/joycevan...
